warmup.examiner: Hello. My name's Anna. What's your name?

part1.title: Find the Differences
part1.scene: Two pictures of an outdoor picnic scene near a lake (tent, bikes, blanket)
part1.intro: Here are two pictures. My picture is similar to yours, but there are some differences.
part1.example: For example, in my picture there's a girl on a rock in the lake, but in your picture there isn't.
part1.instruction: I will say something about my picture. Can you tell me how your picture is different?

questions.examiner: In my picture, two boys are sitting under a tree.
questions.expected: In my picture, two girls are sitting under a tree.
questions.prompt: Who is sitting under a tree in your picture?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the woman is eating a sandwich.
questions.expected: In my picture, the woman is eating pizza.
questions.prompt: What is the woman eating?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there isn't a pizza on the blanket.
questions.expected: In my picture there is a pizza on the blanket.
questions.prompt: What's on the blanket?

questions.examiner: In my picture, two boys are riding their bikes.
questions.expected: In my picture, one boy is riding his bike.
questions.prompt: How many boys are riding their bikes?

questions.examiner: In my picture, a man is reading a newspaper outside the tent.
questions.expected: In my picture, a man is reading a magazine outside the tent.
questions.prompt: What is the man outside the tent reading?

part2.title: Information Exchange
part2.context: Richard and Harry are friends who go to school together.
part2.intro: Richard and Harry are friends. They go to school together. I don't know anything about Richard's programme. So I'm going to ask you some questions.

phaseA.examiner: What time does he get up in the morning?
What time does he go to school?
What does he do at 4:00 p.m.?
What does he do at 7:00 p.m.?
What time does he go to bed?

part2.transition: Now, you don't know anything about Harry's programme, so you ask me some questions.

phaseB.expected_question: What time does he get up?
What time does he go to school?
What does he do at 2 o'clock?
What does he do at 6 p.m.?
What time does he go to bed?

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.intro: These pictures tell a story. It's called 'Betty's visit to London'. Look at the pictures first.
part3.pic1: Betty, her brother Fred and their parents visit London. They arrive there on Monday and they go for a walk. They see Big Ben.

pictures.prompt: What day is it? What does the family do?

sentences.text: On Tuesday Betty and her family go shopping.
sentences.hint: What day is it? What do they do?

sentences.text: They buy many presents and souvenirs.

pictures.prompt: What day is it? What is Betty doing?

sentences.text: On Wednesday Betty writes a postcard to her grandmother.
sentences.hint: What day is it? What does Betty do?

pictures.prompt: What day is it? Where is the family? What do they see? Is Betty afraid of the snakes? Who laughs?

sentences.text: On Thursday the family visits the zoo.
sentences.hint: What day is it? Where do they go?

sentences.text: They see a lot of animals there.
sentences.hint: What do they see at the zoo?

sentences.text: Betty is afraid of the snakes and her brother Fred laughs.
sentences.hint: Is Betty afraid of the snakes? Who laughs?

pictures.prompt: What day is it? Where is the family? What have they got with them? What does the father do?

sentences.text: On Friday they are at the airport.
sentences.hint: What day is it? Where are they?

sentences.text: It is the last day in London.
They have their bags with them.
sentences.hint: What have they got with them?

sentences.text: Betty's father takes pictures of the family.

part4.title: Personal Questions
part4.intro: Now let's talk about what you do in your free time.

questions.examiner: What do you do after school?
How often do you go out with your friends?
What do you usually do at the weekend?
Do you play any sports?
What other things do you do in your free time?
questions.sample: I sometimes go to the cinema.

followups.examiner: Do you play basketball or football?
Do you go to the cinema?
Do you play computer games with your friends?
Do you help with the housework?

part1.title: Find the Differences
part1.scene: Two pictures of a fire station
part1.intro: Here are two pictures. My picture is similar to yours, but there are some differences.
part1.example: For example, in my picture two firemen are drinking coffee, but in your picture they are eating.
part1.instruction: I will say something about my picture. Can you tell me how your picture is different?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the man who is going to the toilet is wearing a white shirt.
questions.expected: In my picture, the man who is going to the toilet is wearing a black shirt.
questions.prompt: Is the man wearing a white shirt?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the driver in the fire engine has got a beard.
questions.expected: In my picture, the driver has got a moustache.
questions.prompt: Has the driver got a beard?

questions.examiner: In my picture, a man is going up the stairs.
questions.expected: In my picture, the man is going down the stairs.
questions.prompt: Is the man going up the stairs?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there is a restaurant next to the fire station.
questions.expected: In my picture, there is a bookshop next to the fire station.
questions.prompt: Is there a restaurant next to the fire station?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there is a motorbike near the trees.
questions.expected: In my picture, there is a bike near the trees.
questions.prompt: Is there a motorbike near the trees?

part2.title: Information Exchange
part2.context: Mr Brown and Mrs Silver are two teachers.
part2.intro: Mr Brown and Mrs Silver are two teachers. I don't know anything about Mr Brown, but you do. So I'm going to ask you some questions.

phaseA.examiner: How many years has he been a teacher?

part2.transition: Now, you don't know anything about Mrs Silver, so you ask me some questions.

phaseB.expected_question: How many years has she been a teacher?

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.intro: These pictures tell a story. It's called 'Michael's dream'. Look at the pictures first.
part3.pic1: Michael is very tired. He brushes his teeth and he's getting ready to go to bed.

pictures.prompt: What is Michael doing?

sentences.text: Michael is sleeping and he is dreaming.
sentences.hint: What is Michael doing in bed?

sentences.text: In his dream, he is in a desert and he is riding a camel.
sentences.hint: What is happening in his dream?

sentences.text: It's very hot and Michael is very thirsty.
sentences.hint: How is the weather? How does Michael feel?

pictures.prompt: Where is Michael now?

sentences.text: Michael is now on a big bird.
Michael is flying in the sky on the bird and he is excited.
sentences.hint: What is he doing on the bird? How does he feel?

pictures.prompt: Where is Michael? What does he want to do?

sentences.text: Michael is in space now.
He is going to the moon in a rocket.
He is ready to get off the rocket and walk on the moon.
sentences.hint: What does he want to do on the moon?

pictures.prompt: What happens to Michael? Why?

sentences.text: Michael falls off the bed and wakes up.
He was dreaming.

part4.title: Personal Questions
part4.intro: Now, let's talk about what you do in the summer.

questions.examiner: Where do you like going for your holidays?
What time do you usually get up?
Who do you spend your summer with?
What do you like doing in the summer?
Tell me about other things you do in the summer.

followups.examiner: Do you spend time with your friends?
Do you look for shells on the beach?

part1.title: Find the Differences
part1.scene: Two pictures of a countryside scene (farm, lake, bridge, truck)
part1.intro: Here are two pictures. My picture is similar to yours, but there are some differences.
part1.example: For example, in my picture the boy who is carrying the bottles has longer hair, but in your picture he has shorter hair.
part1.instruction: I will say something about my picture. Can you tell me how your picture is different?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the girl who is cleaning the table is wearing glasses.
questions.expected: In my picture, the girl isn't wearing glasses.
questions.prompt: Is the girl wearing glasses?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there are two rabbits behind the tree.
questions.expected: In my picture, there are three rabbits behind the tree.
questions.prompt: How many rabbits are there behind the tree?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there is a swan in the lake.
questions.expected: In my picture, there is a duck in the lake.
questions.prompt: Is there a swan in the lake?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the woman on the bridge is wearing a ring.
questions.expected: In my picture, the woman isn't wearing a ring.
questions.prompt: Is the woman wearing a ring?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there is a driver in the truck.
questions.expected: In my picture, there isn't a driver in the truck.
questions.prompt: Is there a driver in the truck?

part2.title: Information Exchange
part2.context: Robert and William are brothers who each have a pet.
part2.intro: Robert and William are brothers. They have got two pets. I don't know anything about Robert's pet, but you do. So I'm going to ask you some questions.
part2.transition: Now, you don't know anything about William's pet, so you ask me some questions.

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.intro: These pictures tell a story. It's called 'A hungry bird'. Look at the pictures first.
part3.pic1: Betty and her mum are making a cake.

pictures.prompt: What is Betty doing? What is her mother doing? Where is the parrot?

sentences.text: Betty and her mum are in the kitchen.
sentences.hint: Where are Betty and her mum?

sentences.text: Betty's mum is putting some flour in a bowl and Betty is taking the eggs out of the fridge.
sentences.hint: What are Betty and her mum doing?

sentences.text: Their pet parrot is in a cage.

pictures.prompt: Who is in the kitchen? What is Betty doing now?

sentences.text: Betty is holding the bowl.
Her mother is looking for something in a cupboard.
A boy is in the kitchen and is giving some water to the parrot.
sentences.hint: Who else is in the kitchen? What is he doing?

pictures.prompt: Where is the parrot now? Where is the butter?

sentences.text: The boy leaves the room but he has left the door of the cage open.
sentences.hint: What did the boy forget to do?

sentences.text: The parrot has come out of the cage.
Betty and her mother don't see that.
sentences.hint: Do Betty and her mother notice?

sentences.text: The parrot is eating the butter.

pictures.prompt: Where is the chocolate? Is the parrot in the cage?

sentences.text: The parrot has taken the chocolate to its cage.
sentences.hint: What has the parrot done with the chocolate?

sentences.text: Betty and her mother can't find anything on the table.
sentences.hint: What do Betty and her mother see on the table?

pictures.prompt: Where is the parrot? What has it eaten? Are Betty and her mother happy?

sentences.text: Betty is trying to see where the parrot is and she sees it in the cage.
sentences.hint: Where does Betty find the parrot?

sentences.text: It's just eaten all the chocolate.
Betty and her mother are both angry.
sentences.hint: How do Betty and her mother feel?

questions.examiner: What time do you get there?
How do you get to school?
What's your favourite subject?
What sports do you play at school?
Tell me about other things you do at school.
questions.sample: I spend time with my friends in the playground.

followups.examiner: Do you spend time with your friends?
Do you have lunch at school?

part1.title: Find the Differences
part1.scene: Two pictures of a fishing trip (boat, boys, fish)
part1.intro: Here are two pictures. My picture is similar to yours, but there are some differences.
part1.example: For example, in my picture the man is holding an octopus, but in your picture he's holding a fish.
part1.instruction: I will say something about my picture. Can you tell me how your picture is different?

questions.examiner: In my picture, it's windy and it's raining.
questions.expected: In my picture it isn't raining.
questions.prompt: What's the weather like?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the first boy has got a knife in his pocket.
questions.expected: In my picture, he has got a fork in his pocket.
questions.prompt: What has he got in his pocket?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the third boy is carrying a rucksack.
questions.expected: In my picture, he isn't carrying a rucksack.
questions.prompt: What is the boy carrying?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the third boy isn't holding anything.
questions.expected: In my picture, the third boy is holding a map.
questions.prompt: What is the boy holding?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there are four fish in the boat.
questions.expected: In my picture, there are five fish.
questions.prompt: How many fish are there in the boat?

part2.title: Information Exchange
part2.context: Helen and Katy are best friends, each with a favourite toy.
part2.intro: Helen and Katy are best friends. They have got a favourite toy each. I don't know anything about Helen's favourite toy, but you do. So I'm going to ask you some questions.

phaseA.expected: Her mum bought it for her.

phaseA.examiner: How long has she had it?

part2.transition: Now, you don't know anything about Katy's favourite toy, so you ask me some questions.

phaseB.answer: She's always wanted to have a pet.

phaseB.expected_question: How long has she had it?

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.intro: These pictures tell a story. It's called 'Harry's castle'. Look at the pictures first.
part3.pic1: Harry has to make a castle for a school project.

pictures.prompt: What is the teacher doing?

sentences.text: Harry's teacher is holding a castle and tells the students to make a castle, too.
sentences.hint: What is the teacher showing? What does she tell the students?

pictures.prompt: Where is Harry now? How does he feel? What is his dad carrying?

sentences.text: Harry goes back home.
He is in the garage and he is thinking how to make the castle.
sentences.hint: Where is he? What is he thinking about?

sentences.text: He is sad.
His dad comes in the garage carrying a box.
sentences.hint: Who comes in? What is he carrying?

pictures.prompt: Is Harry sad now? What is he thinking about?

sentences.text: Harry has an idea.
sentences.hint: Does Harry have a plan now?

sentences.text: He is going to ask his father to help him.
sentences.hint: Who is he going to ask for help?

sentences.text: They are going to use the box, some glue and some coloured pencils to make the castle.
sentences.hint: What will they use to make the castle?

pictures.prompt: Is the castle ready? Does Harry like it? What is Harry's dad putting on the castle?

sentences.text: His dad is helping him.
The castle is ready and Harry is very happy now.
sentences.hint: Is the castle ready? How does Harry feel?

sentences.text: Harry's dad is putting a flag on the castle.
sentences.hint: What is his dad putting on the castle?

pictures.prompt: Do Harry's friends like the castle?

sentences.text: Harry takes the castle to school and everyone likes it.

part4.title: Personal Questions
part4.intro: Now, let's talk about the future.

questions.examiner: What job would you like to do?
How old are you going to be when you get married?
Where would you like to live?
How many children would you like to have?

part1.title: Find the Differences
part1.scene: Two pictures of an indoor scene with a table, plant, and a woman
part1.intro: Here are two pictures. My picture is similar to yours, but there are some differences.
part1.example: For example, in my picture there are five plates on the table, but in your picture there are six plates.
part1.instruction: I will say something about my picture. Can you tell me how your picture is different?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the bottle of water is full.
questions.expected: In my picture, the bottle is empty.
questions.prompt: Is there any water in the bottle?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there are flowers on the table.
questions.expected: In my picture, there are sweets on the table.
questions.prompt: What is on the table?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there is a pair of scissors under the table.
questions.expected: In my picture, there is a belt under the table.
questions.prompt: What is there under the table?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there is an insect on the plant.
questions.expected: In my picture, there are two insects on the plant.
questions.prompt: What's on the plant in your picture?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the woman is wearing a spotted dress.
questions.expected: In my picture, the woman is wearing a striped dress.
questions.prompt: What kind of dress is the woman wearing?

part2.title: Information Exchange
part2.context: Betty and Helen are friends who each went on a holiday.
part2.intro: Betty and Helen are friends. They took some pictures of their last holiday. I don't know anything about Betty's holiday, but you do. So I'm going to ask you some questions.
part2.transition: Now, you don't know anything about Helen's holiday, but I do. So you are going to ask me some questions.

phaseB.answer: She went for walks in the forest.

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.intro: These pictures tell a story. It's called 'Family shopping'. Look at the pictures first.
part3.pic1: Richard and his parents go to a shop. There are bookcases there.

pictures.prompt: What is Richard's mum looking at? How does she feel about them? What do you think she wants to do?

sentences.text: Richard's mum is looking at some silver spoons, forks and knives and she likes them.
sentences.hint: What is Richard's mum looking at? Does she like them?

sentences.text: I think she wants to buy them.
sentences.hint: What do you think she wants to do?

pictures.prompt: Where are the people now? What are these things made of?

sentences.text: Now the family is in a room with things made of paper.
sentences.hint: Where are they now? What are things made of?

sentences.text: For example, there are lots of boxes, envelopes and books.

pictures.prompt: What happens then? What are these made of? What does Richard do? How does he feel?

sentences.text: Now the family is looking at some things made of glass, like vases.
sentences.hint: What are they looking at now? What are these made of?

sentences.text: Richard drops one.
He looks very unhappy.

pictures.prompt: What happens in the end?

sentences.text: In the end, the glass breaks.

questions.examiner: What's your favourite food?
What time do you have breakfast?
How often do you eat chocolate?
What do you usually have for dinner?
Now tell me what other food you like eating.

followups.examiner: Do you like pasta or chicken for lunch?

part1.title: Find the Differences
part1.scene: Two pictures of a museum/dinosaur scene with a tent, fire, and tiger display
part1.intro: Here are two pictures. My picture is similar to yours, but there are some differences.
part1.example: For example, in my picture there are two fish, but in your picture there are three.
part1.instruction: I will say something about my picture. Can you tell me how your picture is different?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the dinosaur's tail is long.
questions.expected: In my picture, the dinosaur's tail is short.
questions.prompt: Is the dinosaur's tail long?

questions.examiner: In my picture, I can see a torch in the boy's backpack.
questions.expected: In my picture, I can see a bottle in his backpack.
questions.prompt: Is there a torch in the boy's backpack?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the sky is yellow.
questions.expected: In my picture, the sky is blue.
questions.prompt: What colour is the sky?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the spider is below the tiger.
questions.expected: In my picture, it's above the tiger.
questions.prompt: Where's the spider?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the door of the tent is open.
questions.expected: In my picture, the door of the tent is closed.
questions.prompt: Is the door of the tent open?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there are two stones around the fire.
questions.expected: In my picture, there are three stones around the fire.
questions.prompt: How many stones are there around the fire?

part2.title: Information Exchange
part2.context: Harry and Helen have new books to read.
part2.intro: Harry and Helen have new books to read. I don't know anything about Harry's new book, but you do. So I'm going to ask you some questions.

phaseA.examiner: What's the name of Harry's new book?
How many pages has the book got?
Is the book funny or sad?

part2.transition: Now you don't know anything about Helen's new book, so you ask me some questions.

phaseB.expected_question: What's the name of Helen's book?
How many pages has the book got?
Is the book funny or sad?

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.intro: These pictures tell a story. It's called 'Mum and the fruit salad'. Look at the pictures first.
part3.pic1: Ben's mum wants to make fruit salad. She's just bought a lot of fruit. Now they're going home.

pictures.prompt: What's happening to the fruit? What is the boy doing?

sentences.text: The fruit is falling out of Mum's bags.
A boy is picking up the fruit.

pictures.prompt: What's Mum saying? What can Ben see?

sentences.text: Mum's saying, 'Where's my fruit?'
Ben can see some people with the fruit.

pictures.prompt: Has Mum got her fruit now? What's she saying?

sentences.text: The people are giving the fruit to Mum and Ben.
sentences.hint: Has Mum got her fruit now?

sentences.text: Mum's saying, 'Thank you.'

pictures.prompt: What's Mum doing? And the other people?

sentences.text: Mum's making fruit salad.
Everyone's eating it.
sentences.hint: What are the other people doing?

part4.title: Personal Questions
part4.intro: Now let's talk about your city, town or village.

questions.examiner: Where do you live?
How do you go to school?
What's your favourite place in your city/town/village?
Where do you go shopping?
questions.sample: I go shopping in the town centre.

questions.examiner: Tell me what you do at the weekends in your city/town/village.
questions.sample: I go to the cinema. I go to a restaurant with my family.

followups.examiner: Do you live in a city or a village?
Do you go to school by car?
Do you like the sports centre?
Do you go to the cinema?

followups.sample: Yes, I play with my friends.

part1.title: Find the Differences

part1.intro: Here are two pictures. My picture is similar to yours, but there are some differences.

questions.expected: In my picture, the tortoise is under the desk.

questions.expected: In my picture, the robot's sitting on the shelf.

questions.examiner: In my picture, there's a castle outside the window.
questions.expected: In my picture, there's a bridge outside the window.

questions.examiner: In my picture, there's a message on the computer screen.
questions.expected: In my picture, there's a photo on the computer screen.

questions.examiner: In my picture, the bin is on the right.
questions.expected: In my picture, the bin is on the left.

part2.title: Information Exchange
part2.context: Robert and Vicky have both got a present.
part2.intro: Robert and Vicky have both got a present. I don't know anything about Robert's present, but you do. So I'm going to ask you some questions.

phaseA.examiner: When did Robert get his present?

part2.transition: Now you don't know anything about Vicky's present, so you ask me some questions.

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.intro: These pictures tell a story. It's called 'Peter and Charlie get lost'. Look at the pictures first.
part3.pic1: Two brothers, Peter and Charlie, are camping. They decide to go for a long walk in the jungle.

pictures.prompt: Where are Peter and Charlie? What's the monkey doing?

sentences.text: Peter and Charlie are in the jungle.
A monkey's throwing bananas to them.

pictures.prompt: What are the boys doing now? Are the boys lost?

sentences.text: The boys are looking at a map.
They're lost.

pictures.prompt: What can the monkey see?

sentences.text: The monkey can see the boys' tent.

pictures.prompt: Where's the monkey taking the boys? Are they happy?

sentences.text: The monkey's taking the boys back to their tent.
sentences.hint: Where's the monkey taking the boys?

sentences.text: They're happy.

questions.examiner: What do you have for breakfast?
Where do you usually eat at home?
What time do you have lunch?
What food don't you like?
Tell me about dinner in your home.
questions.sample: My mum cooks dinner. We have chicken and rice. We eat at 8 o'clock.

followups.examiner: Do you usually eat in the kitchen?
Do you have lunch at one o'clock?
What time do you eat dinner?

part1.title: Find the Differences
part1.scene: Two pictures of an airport scene
part1.intro: Here are two pictures. My picture is similar to yours, but there are some differences.
part1.example: For example, in my picture a dog is at the back of the truck, but in your picture it's at the front.
part1.instruction: I will say something about my picture. Can you tell me how your picture is different?

questions.examiner: In my picture, one person is on the steps of the plane.
questions.expected: In my picture, three people are on the steps of the plane.
questions.prompt: How many people are on the steps?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the blue plane is going up into the sky.
questions.expected: In my picture, the blue plane is coming down.

questions.examiner: In my picture, the woman in the uniform is wearing a green scarf.
questions.expected: In my picture, the woman's scarf is orange.

questions.examiner: In my picture, the green number above the plane is 24.
questions.expected: In my picture, the number is 57.
questions.prompt: What's the number above the plane?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the man's suitcase has got wheels on it.
questions.expected: In my picture, the man's suitcase hasn't got wheels on it.

questions.examiner: In my picture, I can see an ambulance near the skyscrapers.

part2.title: Information Exchange
part2.context: Michael and Emma go to a sports club every week.
part2.intro: Michael and Emma go to a sports club every week. I don't know anything about Michael's sports club, but you do. So I'm going to ask you some questions.

phaseA.examiner: What sport does Michael do there?
Which day does Michael go there?

part2.transition: Now you don't know anything about Emma's sports club, so you ask me some questions.

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.story_name: Tom and Betty go to the hospital
part3.intro: These pictures tell a story. It's called 'Tom and Betty go to the hospital'. Look at the pictures first.
part3.pic1: Tom and Betty's mum is in hospital because she's broken her leg. Tom and Betty and their dad are going to the hospital to visit her.

pictures.prompt: What's Tom doing?

sentences.text: Tom's giving his mum a present.
Mum's smiling.

pictures.prompt: What is the present?

sentences.text: Mum's opened her present.
She's got some chocolates.

pictures.prompt: What's Betty doing? And what's Tom doing?

sentences.text: Betty's taking the chocolates.
Tom's telling his parents but they aren't listening.

pictures.prompt: Why is Tom angry?

sentences.text: Tom's angry because Betty's eaten all the chocolates.
Mum and Dad are laughing.
sentences.hint: What are Mum and Dad doing?

part4.title: Personal Questions
part4.intro: Now let's talk about your school.

questions.examiner: How many children are there in your class?
What colour is your classroom?
What's your favourite lesson?
Where do you eat lunch?
Tell me about your best friend at school.

part1.title: Find the Differences
part1.scene: Two pictures of an outdoor scene with a car, tent, trees, and lake
part1.intro: Here are two pictures. My picture is similar to yours, but there are some differences.
part1.example: For example, in my picture there are two trees, but in your picture there are three.
part1.instruction: I will say something about my picture. Can you tell me how your picture is different?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the car is blue.
questions.expected: In my picture, it's yellow.
questions.prompt: What colour is the car?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there's a bat above the tent.
questions.expected: In my picture, there's a butterfly above the tent.
questions.prompt: Is there a bat above the tent?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the girl is wearing white shorts.
questions.expected: In my picture, the girl is wearing blue shorts.
questions.prompt: Is the girl wearing white shorts?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the girl's holding a toothbrush.
questions.expected: In my picture, she's holding a flag.
questions.prompt: What's the girl holding?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there's a rabbit in the car.
questions.expected: In my picture, there isn't a rabbit in the car.
questions.prompt: Is there a rabbit in the car?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the woman's going into the tent.
questions.expected: In my picture, the woman's coming out of the tent.
questions.prompt: Is the woman going into the tent?

part2.title: Information Exchange
part2.context: Mary's house and Tom's house are on the same street.
part2.intro: Mary's house and Tom's house are on the same street. I don't know anything about Mary's house, but you do. So I'm going to ask you some questions.

phaseA.examiner: How many people live in the house?
What colour is the front door?
Where does Mary do her homework?

part2.transition: Now you don't know anything about Tom's house, so you ask me some questions.

phaseB.expected_question: Is Tom's house big or small?
Where does Tom do his homework?
What colour is the front door?

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.intro: These pictures tell a story. It's called 'Betty's birthday party'. Look at the pictures first.
part3.pic1: It's Betty's birthday today. Her mother's making a cake. Betty's getting some sugar for the cake. She has to stand on a chair.

pictures.prompt: What has happened? What has Betty hurt? Why is Betty going to bed?

sentences.text: Betty has fallen. She has hurt her leg.
Betty can't walk. She's going to bed.
sentences.hint: Why is Betty going to bed?

pictures.prompt: How is she feeling? What is Betty doing now? Who has come to see her?

sentences.text: She's very sad.
Betty's sleeping. Her friends have come to see her.
sentences.hint: What is Betty doing? Who has come?

pictures.prompt: What are Betty's friends giving her? What's her mother bringing? Is it a good party?

sentences.text: Betty's friends are giving her presents.
Her mother's bringing the birthday cake.
Everyone's enjoying the party.

part4.title: Personal Questions
part4.intro: Now let's talk about your school.

questions.examiner: What time do you go to school?
How do you go to school?
What's your favourite lesson?
What sports do you play at school?
Tell me about your best friend at school.
questions.sample: His name's Tom. He's tall. He has short hair.

followups.examiner: Do you go to school at 8 o'clock?
Is he/she tall? Does he/she have short hair?
followups.sample: Yes, he's tall and has short hair.

part1.title: Find the Differences
part1.scene: Two pictures of a zoo or outdoor scene with animals, an island, and ice-cream seller
part1.intro: Here are two pictures. My picture is similar to yours, but there are some differences.
part1.example: For example, in my picture the camel's got a short tail, but in your picture it's got a long tail.
part1.instruction: I will say something about my picture. Can you tell me how your picture is different?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there are stripes on the tent.
questions.expected: In my picture, there are spots on the tent.
questions.prompt: Are there stripes on the tent?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there are four planes.
questions.expected: In my picture, there are five planes.
questions.prompt: How many planes are there?

questions.examiner: In my picture, I can see a man with a white hat. He's selling ice-cream.
questions.expected: In my picture, the man with the white hat is selling burgers.
questions.prompt: What's the man with the white hat selling?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the elephant is behind the trees.
questions.expected: In my picture, the elephant is in front of the trees.
questions.prompt: Where's the elephant?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the woman with the umbrella is talking to a girl.
questions.expected: In my picture, the woman with the umbrella is talking to a boy.
questions.prompt: Who's the woman with the umbrella talking to?

questions.examiner: In my picture, there's a swan on the island.
questions.expected: In my picture, there isn't a swan on the island.
questions.prompt: Is there a swan on the island?

part2.title: Information Exchange
part2.context: Charlie and Emma each get a magazine every week.
part2.intro: Charlie and Emma get a magazine every week. I don't know anything about Charlie's magazine, but you do. So I'm going to ask you some questions.

What's the name of the magazine?
Who buys the magazine for Charlie?

part2.transition: Now you don't know anything about Emma's magazine, so you ask me some questions.

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.intro: These pictures tell a story. It's called 'The key'. Look at the pictures first.
part3.pic1: Sarah and her mum have come home from the shops. Sarah is carrying lots of bags. She drops her door key.

pictures.prompt: Can Mum find the key? What's Sarah saying?

sentences.text: Mum can't find her key.
Sarah's saying, 'Look! That window's open.'

pictures.prompt: What's Sarah doing? What's she climbing?

sentences.text: Sarah's climbing in the window.

sentences.text: Sarah's opening the door from the inside.
sentences.hint: What is Sarah doing at the door?

pictures.prompt: Where was the key? How do they feel?

sentences.text: Mum has found the key. It was in her pocket.
Sarah and her mum are laughing.

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.intro: These pictures tell a story. It's called 'Charlie and the elephant'. Look at the pictures first.
part3.pic1: Charlie is in the garden with his mother. He's eating some fruit and his mother's reading a book.

pictures.prompt: What can Charlie see? What is it doing?

sentences.text: Charlie can see an elephant. It's coming into the garden.

pictures.prompt: What is Charlie giving the elephant? Who has come?

A man from the circus has come to get the elephant.

part1.title: Find the Differences

part1.intro: Here are two pictures. My picture is similar to yours, but there are some differences.

questions.expected: In my picture, the swan's on the right.

questions.examiner: In my picture, the leaves on the biggest tree are brown.
questions.expected: In my picture, the leaves on the biggest tree are yellow.
questions.prompt: What colour are the leaves on the biggest tree?

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.story_name: The aliens go for a ride

part3.pic1: The astronauts want to go home. Some aliens are helping them with their things. Two aliens would like to go with them in their rocket.

pictures.prompt: What are the astronauts doing? Where are two aliens going?

sentences.text: The astronauts are saying goodbye to the aliens. Two aliens are going into the rocket.
sentences.hint: What are the astronauts doing? Where are the aliens going?

pictures.prompt: What are the astronauts doing now?

sentences.text: Now the rocket is going up into space. The astronauts are waving to the aliens on the ground.

pictures.prompt: Where are the astronauts now? What has the boy seen?

sentences.text: Now the astronauts are back on their planet. Lots of people have come to see them.
One boy has seen the aliens inside the rocket.

pictures.prompt: What have the astronauts seen? How do they feel?

sentences.text: The astronauts have seen the two aliens. They're very surprised.
sentences.hint: What have the astronauts seen? How do they feel?

questions.prompt: How many steps are there down to the beach?

questions.examiner: In my picture, no one is sitting in the striped chair.
questions.expected: In my picture, a woman is sitting in the striped chair.
questions.prompt: Is someone sitting in the striped chair?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the bin's empty.
questions.expected: In my picture, the bin's full.
questions.prompt: Is the bin empty?

questions.examiner: In my picture, the girl in the white T-shirt is playing with a ball.
questions.expected: In my picture, she's making a sand castle.
questions.prompt: What's the girl in the white T-shirt doing?

part2.title: Information Exchange
part2.context: Robert and Emma both live in apartments.
part2.intro: Robert and Emma both live in apartments. I don't know anything about Robert's apartment, but you do. So I'm going to ask you some questions.

phaseA.examiner: How many bedrooms has Robert's apartment got?
Has Robert's apartment got a balcony?

part2.transition: Now you don't know anything about Emma's apartment, so you ask me some questions.

phaseB.expected_question: How many bedrooms has the apartment got?
Has the apartment got a balcony?

part3.title: Tell the Story
part3.intro: These pictures tell a story. It's called 'A frog goes to school'. Look at the pictures first.
part3.pic1: Charlie is walking to school. He can see a frog. The frog is jumping into his pocket. Charlie is going to take the frog to school.

pictures.prompt: Where's Charlie now? What's the teacher saying?

sentences.text: Charlie is in his classroom now. The teacher has seen the frog.
She's saying, 'Put that frog outside!'

pictures.prompt: Where's the frog now? Why is it sad?

sentences.text: The frog's outside the school now. It's sad. It's waiting for Charlie.
sentences.hint: Where's the frog now? How does it feel?

pictures.prompt: Has school finished? What's the frog doing?

sentences.text: Charlie's coming out of school. The frog's jumping back into his pocket again.
sentences.hint: Has school finished? What's the frog doing?

pictures.prompt: Where's Charlie going? What's he saying?

sentences.text: Charlie's going home. The frog's on the lake.
Charlie's saying, 'Goodbye, frog!'

questions.examiner: When do you have holidays?
Where do you like going on holiday?
Who do you go on holiday with?
What weather do you like on holiday?
Tell me about what you do on holiday.
questions.sample: We go swimming. We eat in restaurants. We go shopping.

followups.examiner: Do you have holidays in the summer?
Do you like going to the beach?
Do you go with your family?
